result.summary: Found 2 developer surfaces for apostrophecms.com: 1 REST API with OpenAPI spec and 1 npm-distributed CLI; API auth is per-project via API key, bearer token, or session cookie; no public GraphQL or MCP surface found.

apos_api_key.label: ApostropheCMS API key
apos_api_key.setup: In your ApostropheCMS project, configure an API key in the `@apostrophecms/express` module's `apiKeys` option, e.g. in `modules/@apostrophecms/express/index.js` or equivalent project config as shown in [Authentication](https://apostrophecms.com/docs/reference/api/authentication.html) and the [OpenAPI specification extension](https://apostrophecms.com/extensions/openapi-specification). This is a project-issued key, not a central key from apostrophecms.com.

apos_bearer_token.label: ApostropheCMS bearer token
apos_bearer_token.setup: Log into your own ApostropheCMS project via the REST login endpoint described in [Authentication](https://apostrophecms.com/docs/reference/api/authentication.html) / the [core OpenAPI spec](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apostrophecms/apostrophecms-openapi/main/apostrophecms-openapi.yaml): call `POST /@apostrophecms/login/login` with your project user's credentials and use the returned token as `Authorization: Bearer ...`. This token is issued by each ApostropheCMS project instance.

apos_session_cookie.label: ApostropheCMS session cookie
apos_session_cookie.setup: Log into your own ApostropheCMS project via `POST /@apostrophecms/login/login` with `"session": true` as documented in the [core OpenAPI spec](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apostrophecms/apostrophecms-openapi/main/apostrophecms-openapi.yaml). The project sets a session cookie named after your project shortname, e.g. `project-shortname.sid`; use that cookie on subsequent API calls. This is instance-specific.
